title: ShopElite Ecommerce UI Kit
seoTitle: ShopElite Ecommerce UI Kit - Downloadable Premium UI Template | Multiverse
metaDescription: A storefront-first Next.js ecommerce starter with premium merchandising, featured products, conversion-focused sections, responsive layout, and supporting UI state.
summary: A storefront-first Next.js ecommerce starter with premium merchandising, featured products, conversion-focused sections, responsive layout, and supporting UI state.
description: Built from the mini0 project inside Multiverse, this template packages a polished ecommerce UI system with a storefront home page, category discovery, featured merchandising, timed deals, trust sections, testimonials, newsletter capture, header and footer patterns, and reusable component primitives.
style: Bright conversion-led storefront with premium merchandising
audience: store founders and frontend product teams
prompt: Design a premium ecommerce storefront for a modern retail brand. Include a trust-heavy header, hero, featured products, category discovery, timed deals, product grid, testimonials, newsletter capture, and responsive storefront interactions. Keep the system bright, polished, and conversion-aware across desktop and mobile.
layoutNotes: The homepage opens with merchandising-first hero framing and quickly moves into trust and product proof.
The template keeps section spacing consistent so retail content feels like one system instead of disconnected blocks.
The included file bundle focuses on reusable storefront UI rather than shipping the original API-backed homepage unchanged.
responsiveNotes: The header compresses into mobile-friendly search and menu interactions.
Product and category grids collapse cleanly into stacked cards on smaller screens.
Shared spacing utilities keep the storefront readable across desktop, tablet, and mobile.

files.content:
# ShopElite

ShopElite is a full-stack ecommerce demo built with Next.js 16, React 19, Tailwind CSS 4, Prisma, SQLite, Zustand, and Framer Motion.

This README is written for a new developer opening the project for the first time.

## What This Project Includes

- Storefront home page with hero, featured products, categories, deals, reviews, and newsletter sections
- Product listing with search, sorting, and client-side filtering
- Cart and wishlist UI
- Sign in and sign up pages
- Admin/dashboard pages
- App Router API routes for products, categories, auth, cart, orders, reviews, search, wishlist, and dashboard
- SQLite database via Prisma

## Tech Stack

- Next.js 16
- React 19
- TypeScript 5
- Tailwind CSS 4
- Prisma ORM
- SQLite
- NextAuth
- Zustand
- Framer Motion
- shadcn/ui

## Quick Start

### 1. Requirements

- Node.js 18+ or newer
- npm

### 2. Install dependencies

```bash
npm install
```

### 3. Create `.env`

Create a file named `.env` in the project root with:

```env
DATABASE_URL="file:../db/custom.db"
NEXTAUTH_SECRET="change-this-in-real-projects"
NEXTAUTH_URL="http://localhost:3000"
```

Notes:

- The app uses the SQLite database file at `db/custom.db`.
- Because Prisma resolves the SQLite path from `prisma/schema.prisma`, the correct value is `file:../db/custom.db`.
- A `db/custom.db` file already exists in this workspace.
- There is no `.env.example` file yet, so create `.env` manually.

### 4. Generate Prisma client

```bash
npx prisma generate
```

This is required before running a production build.

### 5. Start the app

```bash
npm run dev
```

Open [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000).

## Daily Commands

```bash
npm run dev
npm run lint
npm run build
npm run start
```

Database commands:

```bash
npm run db:generate
npm run db:push
npm run db:migrate
npm run db:reset
```

## Recommended First-Time Setup

If you want a clean setup from scratch:

```bash
npm install
npx prisma generate
npx prisma db push
npm run dev
```

If you want to use the existing local SQLite database already in the repo, the minimum setup is:

```bash
npm install
npx prisma generate
npm run dev
```

## Production Check

Before shipping changes, run:

```bash
npm run lint
npm run build
```

The project was recently verified to pass both.

## Authentication Notes

- Auth uses NextAuth credentials login
- Password hashing now uses a local crypto-based helper in `src/lib/password.ts`
- Seeded users in `prisma/seed.ts` do not include passwords
- That means seeded users are useful as database demo data, but not as ready-to-use login accounts

For login testing:

- Use the sign-up page to create a real account with a password
- Or manually create/update a user record with a hashed password if you are testing admin/auth flows directly

## Database Notes

Prisma schema lives in [prisma/schema.prisma](/C:/projects/mini0/prisma/schema.prisma).

Main models:

- `User`
- `Category`
- `Product`
- `Cart` and `CartItem`
- `Order` and `OrderItem`
- `Review`
- `Address`
- `WishlistItem`
- `Coupon`
- `Setting`

Important details:

- Database provider is SQLite
- Product `images`, `tags`, and `attributes` are stored as JSON strings in the database and parsed in API responses
- The app uses Prisma client from `src/lib/db.ts`

## Project Structure

```text
prisma/
  schema.prisma
  seed.ts
db/
  custom.db
src/
  app/
    api/
    auth/
    checkout/
    dashboard/
    layout.tsx
    page.tsx
    globals.css
  components/
    auth/
    checkout/
    dashboard/
    ecommerce/
    layout/
    ui/
  hooks/
  lib/
    db.ts
    password.ts
    store.ts
    types.ts
    utils.ts
public/
package.json
next.config.ts
```

## Main App Areas

### Storefront

- Home page entry: `src/app/page.tsx`
- Shared section spacing and layout helpers: `src/app/globals.css`
- Header and footer: `src/components/layout/`
- Ecommerce sections: `src/components/ecommerce/`

### State

Zustand stores live in `src/lib/store.ts`.

Key stores:

- cart store
- auth store
- UI store
- product filter store

### API

API routes live in `src/app/api/`.

Important endpoints:

- `/api/products`
- `/api/products/[slug]`
- `/api/categories`
- `/api/auth/register`
- `/api/auth/[...nextauth]`
- `/api/cart`
- `/api/orders`
- `/api/reviews`
- `/api/search`
- `/api/wishlist`
- `/api/dashboard`

### Styling

- Tailwind CSS is used everywhere
- Shared section utilities are defined in `src/app/globals.css`
- Recent UI cleanup standardized section width, padding, spacing, and centered headers

## Known Practical Notes

- `npm run build` requires Prisma client generation first
- If `prisma generate` fails, it is usually because Prisma needs to download engine files
- `npm run dev` uses port `3000`
- `npm run start` also runs on port `3000`
- `next.config.ts` currently ignores TypeScript build errors during production builds with `typescript.ignoreBuildErrors = true`
- Lint should still be treated as required

## Seeding Data

There is a seed file at `prisma/seed.ts`, but there is currently no npm script wired for it.

Before using it, check that your preferred TypeScript runner is available. Common options are:

```bash
npx tsx prisma/seed.ts
```

or

```bash
npx ts-node prisma/seed.ts
```

Important:

- The seed clears existing data first
- It creates categories, products, sample order data, and demo users
- Demo users do not get passwords

If you only want to browse the app locally, you do not need to run the seed if `db/custom.db` already contains data.

## Suggested New-User Workflow

1. Install dependencies with `npm install`
2. Create `.env`
3. Run `npx prisma generate`
4. Start the app with `npm run dev`
5. Open the homepage and confirm products are visible
6. Create a fresh account from the sign-up page if you want to test auth
7. Run `npm run lint` before making larger changes
8. Run `npm run build` before handing work off

## Troubleshooting

### Build fails with Prisma client not initialized

Run:

```bash
npx prisma generate
```

### App starts but auth login does not work for seeded users

That is expected if those users were created without passwords. Create a new user through sign-up.

### No products appear

Check:

- `.env` points to `file:./db/custom.db`
- `.env` points to `file:../db/custom.db`
- the `db/custom.db` file exists
- the database actually contains product rows

### Prisma command fails while downloading engines

Try again with network access enabled. Prisma may need to fetch platform-specific binaries the first time.

## Files Worth Reading First

- [package.json](/C:/projects/mini0/package.json)
- [src/app/page.tsx](/C:/projects/mini0/src/app/page.tsx)
- [src/app/globals.css](/C:/projects/mini0/src/app/globals.css)
- [src/lib/store.ts](/C:/projects/mini0/src/lib/store.ts)
- [src/lib/db.ts](/C:/projects/mini0/src/lib/db.ts)
- [src/lib/password.ts](/C:/projects/mini0/src/lib/password.ts)
- [prisma/schema.prisma](/C:/projects/mini0/prisma/schema.prisma)

## Current Status

At the time of this README update:

- `npm run lint` passes
- `npm run build` passes
- local runtime check on `/` returned HTTP 200

files.summary: Shared app layout, fonts, metadata, and toaster shell.
Global storefront tokens, spacing, and utility styles.

{mainImage && !imageError ? ( setImageError(true)} /> ) : (
)}

{product.name}

{product.rating.toFixed(1)}
{formatPrice(product.price)} {discount > 0 && ( -{discount}% )}
) } export function DealsSection({ products }: DealsSectionProps) { const [endTime] = useState(() => { const date = new Date() date.setHours(23, 59, 59, 999) return date }) return (
{/* Left Content */}

{products.slice(0, 4).map((product, index) => ( ))}
) }

)}

getItemCount() : 0 const cartItems = mounted ? items : [] const cartTotal = mounted ? getTotal() : 0 const hydratedUser = mounted ? user : null useEffect(() => { const handleScroll = () => { setScrolled(window.scrollY > 20) } window.addEventListener('scroll', handleScroll) return () => window.removeEventListener('scroll', handleScroll) }, []) const handleSearch = (e: React.FormEvent) => { e.preventDefault() if (searchQuery.trim()) { console.log('Searching:', searchQuery) } } return (

export function getMockCategories(): Category[] {
  return mockCategories.map((category) => ({ ...category }))
}

export function getMockProducts(): Product[] {
  return mockProducts.map((product) => ({
    ...product,
    images: [...product.images],
    tags: [...product.tags],
    attributes: { ...product.attributes },
    category: product.category ? { ...product.category } : undefined,
  }))
}

// Product Types
export interface Product {
  id: string
  name: string
  slug: string
  description: string
  price: number
  comparePrice: number | null
  images: string[]
  categoryId: string | null
  sku: string
  stock: number
  featured: boolean
  trending: boolean
  rating: number
  reviewCount: number
  tags: string[]
  attributes: Record<string, string>
  createdAt: string
  updatedAt: string
  category?: Category
}

export interface Category {
  id: string
  name: string
  slug: string
  description: string | null
  image: string | null
  productCount?: number
}

// Order Types
export interface Order {
  id: string
  orderNumber: string
  userId: string
  status: OrderStatus
  subtotal: number
  tax: number
  shipping: number
  discount: number
  total: number
  currency: string
  shippingAddress: ShippingAddress
  paymentMethod: string | null
  paymentStatus: PaymentStatus
  trackingNumber: string | null
  notes: string | null
  createdAt: string
  items: OrderItem[]
}

export type OrderStatus =
  | 'PENDING'
  | 'PROCESSING'
  | 'SHIPPED'
  | 'DELIVERED'
  | 'CANCELLED'
  | 'REFUNDED'

export type PaymentStatus = 'PENDING' | 'PAID' | 'FAILED' | 'REFUNDED'

export interface OrderItem {
  id: string
  productId: string
  productName: string
  productSku: string
  quantity: number
  price: number
  total: number
  image: string | null
}

export interface ShippingAddress {
  name: string
  phone?: string
  address: string
  city: string
  state: string
  zipCode: string
  country: string
}

// Review Types
export interface Review {
  id: string
  userId: string
  productId: string
  rating: number
  title: string | null
  comment: string | null
  verified: boolean
  helpful: number
  createdAt: string
  user?: {
    name: string | null
    image: string | null
  }
}

// Dashboard Stats
export interface DashboardStats {
  totalRevenue: number
  totalOrders: number
  totalProducts: number
  totalCustomers: number
  revenueGrowth: number
  ordersGrowth: number
  recentOrders: Order[]
  topProducts: Product[]
  salesByMonth: { month: string; revenue: number }[]
}

// User Types
export interface User {
  id: string
  email: string
  name: string | null
  role: 'CUSTOMER' | 'ADMIN'
  image: string | null
}

previewHtml:
ShopElite
FeaturedCategoriesDealsNewsletter

Ecommerce UI starter

Launch a premium storefront without rebuilding the basics.

ShopElite packages a polished retail home page, category discovery, featured merchandising, timed promotions, testimonials, and reusable storefront source files.

View sectionsDownload kit
12+storefront modules inside the kit
Next.jsready for modern commerce MVPs
Mock datashipped for instant local previewing

Includes the actual storefront source bundle from the mini0 project, packaged for Multiverse templates.

Conversion-first layoutResponsive merchandisingCart + auth surfacesDownloadable source bundle

Category discovery

Highlight collections with cleaner retail hierarchy.

Use these blocks for departments, curated drops, or seasonal collections.

Electronics

Smart devices and premium everyday tech.

Fashion

Modern wardrobe staples and elevated basics.

Home & Living

Comfort-focused pieces for contemporary spaces.

Sports & Outdoors

Performance gear for active routines.

Featured products

Merchandise hero products with sharper cards and pricing emphasis.

The kit includes hero, product grid, category, countdown, trust, testimonial, and newsletter sections.

Aurora Headphones

Noise-canceling over-ear headphones with strong hero appeal and premium pricing treatment.

$1994.8 rating

Orbit Smartwatch Pro

Retail-ready product card layout with clear secondary metadata and cleaner spacing.

$279Health + GPS

Canvas Utility Jacket

Balanced card framing built for apparel, electronics, and mixed-category merchandising.

$89Featured

Flash deals module

Timed promotions and callouts are built into the storefront flow.

The source bundle includes the countdown-driven deals section from the original project.

12Hours24Minutes08Seconds

Why teams use it

More than a landing page: a practical commerce starter with supporting surfaces.

Storefront UI

Homepage merchandising, product grids, category cards, testimonials, and newsletter capture.

State + interactions

Cart drawers, filters, mobile navigation, and commerce-friendly components are included in the bundle.

Source bundle

Real Next.js files from the mini0 project so teams can start with actual structure instead of flat snippets.

Newsletter module

Finish the flow with retention and repeat-visit capture.

The kit also includes the storefront footer, trust badges, and call-to-action patterns used throughout the original design.
